THOMPSON IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHALL PROVIDE COMPLETE OBJECTION-FREE RESPONSES AND $2,000 IN SANCTIONS BY JANUARY 23, 2012. EVEN THOUGH PLAINTIFF HAS YET TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT, THE PROPOUNDED DISCOVERY MEETS THE "RELEVANT TO THE SUBJECT MATTER" STANDARD OF CCP 2017.010. SEE STEWART V. COLONIAL WESTERN AGENCY, INC., (2001) 87 CAL.APP.4TH 1006, 1013. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER; NOT REPORTED

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Deborah Kochan, Esq. of Kochan & Stephenson, Tel. 415.392.6200, appearing for the plaintiff and moving party.
Alka Ramchandani, Esq. of Ogletree Deakins, Tel. 415.442.4810, appearing for defendants and opposing party.

The parties did not stipulate for the Motion to be heard by a Judge Pro Tem. After hearing, PLAINTIFF STACEY ZIELENSKI's Motion To Quash Or Narrow Defendants' Subpoena And Request For A Monetary Sanction In The Amount Of $1,535 Against Defendants And Their Counsel Charles Thompson is continued to May 25, 2012 at 9:30 AM in Department 302 before Judge Kahn. Pro Tem Judge: Ernst Halperin, not reported.

Off Calendar Dec-22-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF STACEY ZIELENSKI'S MOTION TO COMPEL VERIFICATIONS FOR DEFENDANTS RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST ROUND OF WRITTEN DISCOVERY AND REQUEST FOR A MONETARY SANCTION AGAINST DEFENDANTS AND THEIR COUNSEL CHARLES L. THOMPSON.
Appearances: Mathew Stephenson of Kochan & Stephenson (392.6200) for plaintiff and moving party; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. Defendant shall provide its verifications and $300 in sanctions by January 23, 2012. Order signed. (302/MJM) Judge: Marla Miller; Not reported

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories And Pltfs'S Second Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Dec-22-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF STACEY ZIELENSKI'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST SET OF SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND PLAINTIFF'S SECOND SET OF REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS TO DEFENDANTS PARC 55 HOTEL MANAGEMENT LLC AND HIGHGATE HOTELS LP AND REQUEST FOR A MONETARY SANCTION AGAINST DEFENDANTS AND THEIR COUNSEL, CHARLES L. THOMPSON.
Appearances: Mathew Stephenson of Kochan & Stephenson (392.6200) for plaintiff and moving party; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. Defendant shall provide complete objection-free responses and $2,000 in sanctions by January 23, 2012. Even though Plaintiff has yet to amend the complaint, the propounded discovery meets the "relevant to the subject matter" standard of CCP 2017.010. See Stewart v. Colonial Western Agency, Inc., (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 1006, 1013. Order signed. (302/MJM) Judge: Marla Miller; Not reported

Notice Of Motion Andm Otion To Compel Further Responses To Pltfs' First Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents Defts And Request For Monetary Sanctions As Against Defts And Their Counsel

THOMPSON.
Appearances: Mathew Stephenson of Kochan & Stephenson (392.6200) for plaintiff and moving party; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. Defendant shall provide its responses and $900 in sanctions by January 23, 2012. The parties are to meet and confer regarding production request #12. Plaintiff may file a new motion if the parties cannot resolve their differences as to that request or any other request. Order signed. (302/MJM) Judge: Marla Miller; Not reported
